stories.title: Germany rebukes Trump over South Africa’s G20 ban
stories.summary: JOHANNESBURG, South Africa — German Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul on Thursday criticized U.S. President Donald Trump’s announcement that he would exclude South Africa from the next G20 summit in Miami, saying it would be “in no one’s interest.” Speaking to students at the University of the Witwatersrand in Johannesburg, Wadephul described South Africa as “an indispensable member of the G20,” whose importance had been underlined by last year’s G20 summit in

stories.title: Pride organisers vow to march in Romanian city that has blocked event four years in a row
stories.summary: Oradea is only major city in EU where local authorities have repeatedly blocked Pride plans Campaigners in a Romanian city have vowed to go ahead with a Pride march after local authorities denied permission for the fourth consecutive year. Since 2023, campaigners in the western city of Oradea, which has a population of about 180,000, have sought permission to hold Pride events. However, each year they have faced opposition, making Oradea the only major city in the EU where local authorities have

stories.title: Czechia: Army helicopter crashes, killing at least one
stories.summary: A Czech army helicopter carrying five troops crashed in eastern Czech Republic, the military said.

stories.summary: Hasonló szakmai feladatok ellátásáért kisebb terhelés mellett lényegesen magasabb jövedelmet kapnak majd.

stories.title: Állami Számvevőszék: Az ÁSZ ellenőrzi a kormányt, nem a kormány az ÁSZ-t
stories.summary: Windisch László elnök Magyar Péter személyét érintő kijelentéseit példátlan kormányzati beavatkozási kísérletként értékeli.

stories.title: A volt megafonos Szűcs Gábor szerint a közösségi média szellemi és erkölcsi kútmérgezést követett el
stories.summary: A fideszes képviselő Dúró Dórával és Schiffer Andrással szidta Magyar Péter abszolút filmszínházát Tusványoson. Schiffer a Tisza Párt sikere mögött azt látja, hogy most lépett szavazókorba a Való Világon felnőtt generáció, amelyet már nem érdekel a szakpolitika.

stories.title: Itt vannak az idei felvételi ponthatárok, az ELTE pszichológiára 455 pont kellett
stories.summary: A jogász képzések ponthatárai minden nagyobb egyetem esetében csökkentek, vagy stagnáltak. Idén az ELTE jogra 467 pontra volt szükség. A BME mechatronikai mérnöki szakjára idén sokkal nehezebb volt bejutni. Rekordot döntött a pedagógusképzésre felvettek száma az ELTE-n, a BGE állami ösztöndíjas hallgatóinak átlagos felvételi pontszáma 415 pont volt.

stories.title: My friends left Ukraine after the Russian invasion. Now I wonder if we live in the same world | Diana Berg
stories.summary: Anger, guilt, shame: the deepest unseen wound of this war is the rift between those who left and those who stayed behind “Can we reschedule?” Over the years of working with western colleagues, I had learned to accept that they don’t fully understand what war feels like from the inside. They would ask me to find someone in occupied Mariupol for an interview, or seem genuinely puzzled that I couldn’t just log on at a time that suited them. I made my peace with this long ago – they don’t have to un

stories.title: A szörnyeteg, aki nem hagyja magát eltörölni
stories.summary: Marilyn Mansont a botrányai miatt kivetette magából a szórakoztatóipar, de a Budapest Parkban adott koncertje alapján a saját mikrouniverzumában még mindig érinthetetlen. A koncert több volt sima nosztalgiázásnál, amin sokat dobott a Manson mögötti zenekar.

stories.title: Magyar Péter Orbán Viktorról: Nagyon nehezen tudom elképzelni, hogy a maffiafőnöknek nem merül fel a felelőssége egy maffiában
stories.summary: A NAV húsz évre visszamenőleg vizsgálhatná politikusok és családtagjaik vagyonosodását. Csökkentik az állami cégek vezetőinek fizetését, 3,8 millió forint lesz a plafon, amit a miniszterelnök mostani jövedelméhez igazítanak. Több feljelentést is tettek a korábban Szijjártó Péter felügyelete alá tartozó Eximbank által nyújtott százmilliárdos hitelek miatt.

stories.title: „Full lopás” – a forrásvesztés elkerülésére lecserélte a Gondosóra programot a kormány az EU helyreállítási alapjánál
stories.summary: Már az Integritás Hatóság vizsgálatának kezdete után, de a múlt heti feljelentés előtt kiszedték az Orbán-kormány digitális idősgondozási programját az uniós helyreállítási tervből.

stories.title: Závecz Research: 74 százalékon áll a Tisza támogatottsága a biztos választók körében
stories.summary: A Fidesz–KDNP-re csak 19 százalék szavazna, ha most lenne a választás. Az elmúlt 36 évben még soha nem fordult elő, hogy egy pártnak 4,5 milliós támogatottsága lett volna.

stories.summary: Az előző szezonban Pécsi Ármin fizikumát fejlesztette a Liverpool, a Hartbergnél fokozatos meccsterhelést kaphat.

stories.title: Az osztrák tiniknek nagyon bejött, a briteknél kemény vitákhoz vezetett, de van köztes megoldás is
stories.summary: 16 lehet az új 18? Nem először merült fel, de most először valós esélye is van, hogy Magyarországon is levigyék a választói korhatárt. Nemzetközi tapasztalat már akad, bár Európában az országok többsége még csak vitázik a kérdésről. A korhatár csökkentése tudatosabb választókat nevel ki, és az elöregedő társadalmakban is fontos szerepe lehet.

stories.summary: A Közterületi Koordinációs Munkacsoport fogja össze a közös munkát.

stories.summary: Sok ajánlást kaptak. A Tisza-frakció több ülést is tarthat a jelöltről. Magyar szerint a politikán kívülről jött tiszás képviselők miatt is érdemes kicsit a megszokott kereteken kívül gondolkodni.

stories.summary: Hungary's former prime minister alleges that his successor has "dismantled the rule of law and eliminated checks and balances." Peter Magyar has pursued Viktor Orban and his allies since winning April's elections.

stories.title: EU’s Google fine risks triggering Trump as he gears up for more tariffs
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — The European Union knew its €890 million fine against Google risked poking the bear in Washington. And the timing couldn’t be worse. President Donald Trump, who’s lashed out at previous EU fines against U.S. tech companies, is in the final hours of deciding a new regime of tariffs, expected when a temporary 10 percent levy is due to expire Friday. That hastily imposed global tax on imports was imposed after the U.S. Supreme Court had struck down Trump’s “Liberation Day”  gl

stories.title: Trump finalizes new tariffs on dozens of countries
stories.summary: The Trump administration on Thursday finalized new double-digit tariffs on dozens of U.S. trading partners as it seeks to reconstitute sweeping duties struck down by the Supreme Court in February. The new duties, which range from 10 to 12.5 percent, follow a five-month investigation into trading partners’ efforts to root out products made with forced labor from their supply chains and are set to take effect just as a temporary global 10 percent tariff expires. stories.title: Donald Trump says decision on ‘massive attack’ against Iran is close
stories.summary: President’s remarks follow Houthi strikes on Saudi tankers in the Red Sea and oil prices rising past $100 a barrel again Middle East crisis live – latest updates Donald Trump has said he is close to deciding whether to launch “a massive attack” against Iran, on a scale larger than strikes already witnessed during the months-long conflict with Tehran. stories.title: India ‘cockroach’ activist Sonam Wangchuk ends hunger strike as protests spread
stories.summary: Sonam Wangchuk halted strike amid concerns about ‘possible violence’ in India as protest movement urges demonstrations across country on Friday over police brutality Indian “cockroach” activist Sonam Wangchuk ended a 26-day hunger strike on Friday over an exam paper scandal that has spiralled into a wideranging youth-led protest against Narendra Modi’s government, with protesters continuing to call for the education minister to resign. Wangchuk said on social media that he ended the hunger strik

stories.title: US threatens 'major military punishment' for Houthi attacks
stories.summary: President Donald Trump threatened to strike Iran and the Houthi militants over attacks on Saudi tankers. Meanwhile, the UN warned that the situation is 'out of control.' DW has the latest.

stories.summary: The "Cockroach" protests which have gained a wider following from Indians of all ages over government accountability, unemployment and economic opportunity is considered one of Prime Minister Narendra Modi's government's biggest challenge yet.

stories.title: ‘Cockroach’ protests: India’s Modi promises to punish officials over exam leaks
stories.summary: PM says ‘those who try to harm the future of our youth will not be spared’ as scandal spirals into widespread anger Narendra Modi has vowed to set up “fast-track courts” to speed up the punishment of officials accused of leaking examination papers, as the Indian prime minister seeks to quell a youth-led “cockroach” protest movement that has rocked Delhi and posed one of the biggest challenges to his government. In his first public remarks on the movement, Modi posted on X: “Nothing is more impor

stories.title: MoD cancels army training in Kenya over powers to prosecute British soldiers
stories.summary: September exercise called off after Kenya raises concerns over funding and jurisdiction for criminal offences The British army has been forced to cancel training for its troops in Kenya after the Kenyan government refused to ratify a bilateral defence agreement. While the UK government wanted to renew the existing agreement, Kenyan lawmakers had sought changes to give local authorities greater powers to hold British soldiers accountable for any offences committed in the country. Continue reading

stories.title: Two Russian men jailed in Angola for terrorism and spying
stories.summary: The men were convicted over alleged plan to stage coup and organise deadly demonstrations over fuel prices last year Two Russian men have been found guilty by an Angolan court of terrorism and spying after being accused of planning a coup and helping to organise deadly demonstrations in the southern African country last year. Lev Lakshtanov and Igor Ratchin were sentenced to eight and 11 years in jail respectively. They had denied the charges, claiming they were in Angola for cultural and busine

stories.title: Israeli security minister joins Jewish extremists as they storm al-Aqsa mosque compound
stories.summary: Far-right minister Itamar Ben-Gvir was among thousands who gathered at Jerusalem’s most sensitive holy site Israel’s far-right minister of national security, Itamar Ben-Gvir, joined thousands of Jewish extremists who stormed Jerusalem’s most sensitive holy site on Thursday. Escorted by armed police, they prayed, waved the Israeli flag and sang the national anthem in a provocative display of political power. Continue reading...

stories.summary: IRGC claims base in south-west England was used by US to launch bombing missions, making it ‘legitimate target’ Middle East crisis live – latest updates The government has said the UK is “ready to defend itself” after Iran’s military warned that any bases being used by the US were “legitimate targets”. The UK has allowed the US to launch “defensive” operations from British bases hosting American planes since the start of its war on Iran but has refused to help in offensive operations. Continue r

stories.title: Double stabbing in New York City being investigated as possible hate crime
stories.summary: Suspect allegedly yelled ‘Allahu Akbar’ before attacking a 57-year-old Asian man and a 50-year-old Jewish man New York City police are investigating a pair of stabbings near Central Park as a possible hate crime, police commissioner Jessica Tisch said Thursday. The suspect, Raul Morales, allegedly stabbed a 57-year-old Asian man in the back with a knife, then stabbed a 50-year-old Jewish man in the torso with a screwdriver, according to a New York police department spokesperson. The Trump administration unveiled the latest tranche of tariffs for countries over their “failure to impose and effectively enforce a prohibition on the importation of goods produced with forced labour”. This, in our view, makes no sense. Forced labour is definitely something which needs

stories.title: Pentagon list of service members killed in Iran war removes most recent four
stories.summary: New York Times reports that list of those killed changed from 18 on Wednesday to 14 on Thursday The Pentagon’s official online list of service members killed in the ongoing Iran war does not include the four soldiers who died during renewed fighting over the weekend, despite the fact that their names were included in a Pentagon press release on the formal transfer of their remains back to the United States on Wednesday. A review of the current list on the Pentagon’s Defense Casualty Analysis Sys

stories.title: EU fines Google €890m for competition breaches over search and apps
stories.summary: Firm told to treat third-party services that appear in its results in ‘fair and non-discriminatory manner’ Google has been fined a total of €890m (£760m) by the EU for breaches of online competition laws by its search and app store services. The European Commission, the EU’s executive arm, said Google had broken the Digital Markets Act by giving priority to its own services, such as shopping and hotel deals, in search results over those of its rivals. Continue reading...

stories.title: Can India's 'Cockroach' protest movement go nationwide?
stories.summary: What began as satire on social media has turned into a massive Gen Z-led protest movement driven by anger over corruption and high youth unemployment. There are signs it is finding traction outside of Delhi.

stories.title: US strikes on southern Iran infrastructure widen war
stories.summary: The latest US strikes on tunnels, bridges and other infrastructure further inland from the Strait of Hormuz could indicate that Washington is shifting gears in Iran.

stories.title: Germany seeks energy and trading partners in Africa
stories.summary: Germany wants to boost relations with Africa. During his trip, German Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul has promoted cooperation on energy, infrastructure and the economy. He also criticized US treatment of South Africa.

stories.summary: Mykhailo Fedorov refuses President Zelensky's offer to accept an alternative post, as the battle of wills continues.

stories.summary: The petition argues the violence against migrants in South Africa could amount to crimes against humanity.

stories.title: You won’t see the back of me before year-end, says ECB’s Lagarde
stories.summary: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde pledged to stay in her role until at least the end of the year, quashing ongoing rumors that she will step down in the near term to take part in France’s 2027 presidential election. Lagarde however did not rule out leaving before the end of her term, which expires in Oct. 2027. The French election will happen in April and May. “I hate to be boxed in in any particular circumstances,” Lagarde told journalists in response to a question f

stories.title: US House votes to end war in Iran, dealing Trump another loss
stories.summary: House lawmakers on Thursday again rebuked President Donald Trump’s handling of the war in Iran, as hostilities flare in the Middle East. Four Republicans broke ranks to help approve a Democratic-led war powers resolution in a 214-208 vote. It was the second time the House has narrowly rejected the renewed war. The Senate could follow suit later this morning in a separate war powers vote. The resolutions are legally untested and largely symbolic. The House measure would not go to President&n

stories.title: Saudis must join Abraham Accords to ensure nuclear deal, Trump says

stories.title: Linnemann wird offenbar neuer Gesundheitsminister
stories.summary: BERLIN – CDU-Generalsekretär Carsten Linnemann soll neuer Bundesgesundheitsminister werden. Die bisherige Amtsinhaberin Nina Warken wechselt in das Kanzleramt. stories.title: France’s far left faces accusations of antisemitism from Olympics DJ
stories.summary: PARIS — Jean-Luc Mélenchon’s far-left France Unbowed party has come under renewed scrutiny over accusations of antisemitism after LGBT+ activist and feminist DJ Barbara Butch was forced to cut short a concert in Grenoble over the weekend. Butch, who is Jewish, plans to file a complaint against Mélenchon’s party for incitement to hatred and violence, her lawyer Audrey Msellati told Franceinfo radio Thursday. stories.title: Owner of Viking ship used in The Odyssey says studio has not paid bill for damage
stories.summary: Swedish nonprofit alleges cost to repair replica was $6,000 but Universal – which made $264m on film’s opening weekend – has not stumped up The Swedish owners of a Viking longship replica that features in the Hollywood blockbuster The Odyssey say they feel “forgotten” after film-makers allegedly returned the vessel damaged and failed to foot the bill for repairs. stories.title: Mayor shot dead in Mexico months after surviving assassination attempt
stories.summary: Valentín Lavín Romero is the 14th mayor to be murdered since Claudia Sheinbaum became president in 2024 A mayor was shot dead on Wednesday in the Mexican state of Morelos after having survived a previous assassination attempt earlier this year. He is the 14th mayor to be murdered since Claudia Sheinbaum became president in October 2024. Valentín Lavín Romero, who was from the Green party, an ally of the ruling leftwing Morena party, was wounded at the end of January when hitmen shot him at a pet

stories.title: Southport killer Axel Rudakubana moved to Broadmoor psychiatric hospital
stories.summary: Exclusive: Clinical assessments concluded Rudakubana could not be safely managed in prison, sources say The Southport killer Axel Rudakubana has been moved from prison to a psychiatric hospital after clinical assessment, the Guardian can disclose. He was transferred to Broadmoor high-security hospital from the category A prison Belmarsh this week after it was concluded that he could not be managed safely in a prison environment, sources confirmed. stories.title: Lobbying firm pressed for details about payments to journalists for favourable stories
stories.summary: MPs and others condemn behaviour of CT Group, which offered cash for positive coverage of clients A political lobbying firm secretly paying journalists for articles favourable to its clients is facing calls to reveal full details of the payments, after the practice was denounced as a dangerous “attack on democracy”. stories.title: Kemi Badenoch ‘has to adapt’ her rhetoric to take on Andy Burnham
stories.summary: Conservative leader is becoming more confident and forging a message for her party, but faces a new opponent “So, Andy Burnham seems like a nice guy.” Those were the slightly unexpected opening words of Kemi Badenoch’s first speech since the change in prime minister. But make no mistake: she was there to bury her opponent. Even Badenoch-sceptics inside the Conservative party and farther afield agree the Tory leader is becoming more confident, and has been able to forge an increasingly coherent m

stories.title: Regional mayors to take key roles in UK policy decisions as part of No 10 North plans
stories.summary: Burnham says ‘no one in government loses when power is shared’ as he revives Brown-era national economic council It’s not just a No 10 in the north, it’s an engine for growth throughout the whole UK | Andy Burnham Regional mayors are to be given a leading role in the UK’s economic policy, Andy Burnham has said, as he called the opening of No 10 North “the biggest rebalancing of power our country has seen”. The new prime minister has revived the national economic council [NEC], first set up by Go

stories.title: Thousands flee wildfires in France and Spain as temperatures reach extreme levels in southern Europe
stories.summary: Spain declares national emergency around Madrid, as marine heatwave sees some parts of Mediterranean Sea 5C hotter than normal Brutal heat torching southern Europe has forced about 30,000 people to flee wildfires in France and Spain as a marine heatwave scalds the Mediterranean and researchers warn that “Dantesque” scenarios cannot be ruled out as infernos worsen. A “particularly intense” wildfire on France’s southwestern Atlantic coast prompted the evacuation of more than 18,800 people, includi

stories.title: Qantas tests new ultra long-range Airbus in 17,000km direct flight from France to Melbourne
stories.summary: Airbus A350–1000ULR becomes most watched plane on flight tracking websites as Qantas tests new aircraft for its Project Sunrise Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Qantas’s new ultra long-distance aircraft has landed on Australian soil for the first time on Friday after a 19-hour test flight from France. The Airbus A350-1000ULR, designed for Qantas to complete some of the longest commercial flights in the world , set

stories.title: Nobody in Europe knows or cares what Kemi Badenoch thinks. That’s a big mistake | Simon Nixon
stories.summary: The opposition leader wants to pull the UK out of the ECHR, wrecking treaties from the Good Friday agreement to the Gibraltar deal Sitting in Brussels, or one of the EU’s 27 chancelleries, one could be forgiven for tuning out the latest psychodramas in Britain’s Conservative party. After all, the country has a new Labour prime minister who is a pro-European keen to build upon the “reset” begun by Keir Starmer . Who cares what a party in opposition thinks about anything when it is still reeling f

stories.title: Personal and banking details among customer data stolen in Origin Energy hack
stories.summary: Hackers access Australian customers’ names, addresses, dates of birth, phone numbers and some bank account details, company says Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Origin Energy customers’ addresses, phone numbers and partial bank account data have been accessed in a hack, the company has confirmed. The firm has 4.8m customer accounts in Australia, providing electricity, fossil gas, LPG and internet services to home

stories.title: Spain's solar eclipse trilogy: spectacle starting August 12
stories.summary: From August 2026 to January 2028, amateur and pro skywatchers are in for a series of rare solar spectacles over Spain and North Africa. Astronomers expect the events to attract millions from around the world.

stories.title: Man dies of injuries after attack at Regensburg bank: police
stories.summary: Police say one person has died after sustaining severe injuries at a bank branch in Regensburg. Special units were dispatched and apprehended a suspect armed with a knife.

stories.title: Trump's refugee policy: Only white South Africans welcome?
stories.summary: Trump is spending more money on refugees, but it's mostly white South Africans who benefit. People from crisis-stricken countries are being denied entry. Experts say the credibility of US refugee aid has been damaged.

stories.title: Is Germany investing in the right weapons?
stories.summary: Germany wants to build up its army and pour hundreds of millions of euros into its defenses. But in a world where the battlefield is increasingly dominated by cheap drones, how many expensive tanks does the country need?

stories.title: US-Saudi nuclear deal: How will it impact Middle East, US-Iran war?
stories.summary: Critics say the US-Saudi deal could spark a nuclear arms race in the Middle East. It's certainly not making US allies, like Israel and the UAE, happy. And reports suggest the US may already be backtracking on the deal.

stories.title: Sweden eases mining permits to cut China minerals reliance
stories.summary: Sweden has declared the mining of critical minerals and rare earths a national security interest. This will ease and expedite the permit process. The government said the step was needed to counter dependence on China.

stories.title: Trump administration drops New York Times subpoenas over Air Force One reporting
stories.summary: The White House had targeted journalists from The New York Times for their reporting on security concerns with a Qatari-donated Air Force One plane.

stories.title: Indian activist Sonam Wangchuk ends hunger strike
stories.summary: Sonam Wangchuk has ended his hunger strike after 26 days, saying the decision follows a "long negotiation" on various conditions and "in view of possible violence in the country."

stories.title: US to impose forced labor tariffs on many trading partners
stories.summary: The Trump administration has unveiled new tariffs on dozens of US trading partners for what it says is lax enforcement of "forced labor" bans. The new tariffs arrive just as a 10% global duty was set to expire.

stories.title: Wildfires in Europe prompt tens of thousands to evacuate
stories.summary: Spain has declared a national emergency in the Madrid region. In neighboring France, meanwhile, President Emmanuel Macron has invoked the EU's civil protection mechanism to ask for help.

stories.title: Who is Ukraine's new military chief?
stories.summary: Mykhailo Drapatyi has been made commander-in-chief of Ukraine's armed forces. His appointment follows a cabinet reshuffle that ousted defense minister Fedorov.

stories.title: US citizens are ramping up pressure on data centers
stories.summary: Rapid data center construction across the US, fueled by AI, comes with serious threats to the environment, public health and energy supply. Local opposition has blocked or delayed dozens of projects.

stories.title: Oil prices jump as Hormuz, Red Sea crises deepen
stories.summary: Renewed strikes on shipping in the Gulf and the Red Sea have dealt a fresh double blow to global oil markets. As prices climb once again, DW examines what else could keep them elevated.

stories.title: Venezuela earthquake damages add up to almost $20 billion, World Bank says
stories.summary: Almost a month after the devastating earthquakes, Venezuela has a mammoth task ahead in terms of rebuilding, rehabilitating and mitigating the social and economic damage of the biggest natural disaster in decades.

stories.title: Liberia seizes €277 million worth of cocaine linked to 'organised and well-funded' trafficking network
stories.summary: According to the Global Initiative Against Transnational Organized Crime, West Africa is a key transit hub for cocaine bound for Europe.

stories.title: Oil prices hit $100 for the first time since May
stories.summary: The price of Brent crude rose more than 6% on Thursday as the war in the Middle East continues to escalate.

stories.title: Nicaragua's legislature debates barring opposition from elections
stories.summary: The move comes days after President Daniel Ortega demanded 'a wall, a blockade' against opposition parties.

stories.title: Ex-governor convicted of murdering a pregnant student in Kenya
stories.summary: Okoth Obado was found guilty of killing Sharon Otieno with whom he had had an affair.

stories.title: Teen jailed for exam cheating in Guinea dies after sickle cell complications, father says
stories.summary: Mamadou Djouma Bah, 17, was arrested and sentenced to prison for exam fraud earlier this month.

stories.title: Coexistence isn’t a medieval memory. Ask the rabbis of the Muslim world
stories.summary: Rabbi Mendy Chitrik argues that Europe should engage more with Jewish communities in Muslim-majority countries, viewing them as living bridges for dialogue, coexistence and cooperation rather than relics of the past.

stories.title: France evacuates 20,000 as wildfires spread in south-west
stories.summary: Most of those evacuated were tourists from campsites around Arcachon Bay in France's Gironde region.

stories.title: White House says Trump will not intervene in Tate brothers' extradition to UK
stories.summary: According to US media, Andrew Tate has also sought to build ties with Trump's sons, Donald Trump Jr. and Barron Trump.

stories.title: Google and Tesla shares plunge as AI spending rattles markets
stories.summary: As major tech companies race to capitalize on a new wave of AI technology, investors wonder when financial benefits will appear.

stories.title: Could you run a country on just three hours of sleep? Japan's PM says she does
stories.summary: Japan’s prime minister Sanae Takaichi says she survives on zero to three hours a night, and people are reacting.

stories.title: Indonesian babies were trafficked to Singapore. What happens to them now?
stories.summary: At least 12 babies have been trafficked from Indonesia to Singapore, but what will happen to them remains unclear.

stories.title: CJP protest: The youth movement India's Modi didn't see coming
stories.summary: The CJP protest began with exam paper leaks. It became India's biggest student movement in years - why?

stories.title: US imposes tariffs on dozens of trade partners over 'forced labour' imports
stories.summary: The tariffs target around 60 trading partners over claims they failed to properly stop forced labour.

stories.title: Houthi attacks raise fears of wider Middle East conflict and more global economic damage
stories.summary: The Iran-backed group in Yemen has threatened to target Saudi vessels in a vital shipping route.

stories.title: Trump links landmark nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia to normalisation with Israel
stories.summary: US Energy Secretary Chris Wright and Saudi Minister of Energy Abdulaziz bin Salman formally signed the agreement, along with "bilateral safeguards" against non-proliferation.

stories.title: Trump says he is 'close to deciding' on a 'massive attack' on Iran
stories.summary: Earlier in the day, Trump threatened the Houthis with "major military punishment" after the Tehran-backed militant group in Yemen claimed on Thursday that it had struck two Saudi oil tankers transiting the Red Sea.

stories.title: 'What was their crime?': BBC visits Iran school where strike killed 120 children
stories.summary: Nearly five months on, grief is still raw as a teacher and a mother recall the horror of the attack in Minab.

stories.title: Opposition parties shut down Indian parliament in solidarity with ‘cockroach’ protests
stories.summary: Proceedings suspended as politicians call for Modi government to address police violence and education scandals India’s opposition parties have shut down parliament in a protest calling for the resignation of the education minister, in solidarity with the new youth-led “cockroach” movement . Opposition politicians, led by the Congress party leader Rahul Gandhi, gathered outside the parliament building in Delhi on Wednesday, many dressed in black and waving placards calling on Dharmendra Pradhan

stories.title: Video shows death of man in Italy while under police restraint - video
stories.summary: Warning: some viewers may find the following footage distressing. Italian police have clashed with demonstrators in Bologna, northern Italy, during a protest over a man's death. The demonstrations on Monday night followed Italian media reports that Abderrahim Fakir, a Moroccan-born businessman, had died on Sunday when police tried to arrest him. A video filmed by a resident and shared on social media shows Fakir repeatedly calling for help as two officers sit on him and ambulance workers appear

stories.title: Trial date set in US drug trafficking case against Nicolas Maduro
stories.summary: The US in April allowed Venezuela to pay for Maduro's and Flores's legal defence, something previously prohibited under US sanctions.

stories.title: Trump threatens to bomb Tehran back for every Iranian attack in Hormuz
stories.summary: The US president said Washington would destroy one piece of civilian infrastructure — including sites near Tehran — each time Iran fires on a ship in the Strait of Hormuz, as the war entered its 11th consecutive day of US strikes.

stories.title: Total solar eclipse books out hotels two years in advance in New Zealand city
stories.summary: Dunedin will be the focal point, having last experienced such an eclipse in 1163 – before any humans reached New Zealand Hotels are booking out two years in advance in the New Zealand city of Dunedin as it prepares for the region’s first total solar eclipse in more than 850 years. At 4.17pm on 22 July 2028, the moon will pass in front of the sun, casting a roughly 100km-wide shadowy band across parts of southern New Zealand. Everywhere within that band – from tourist hotspot Queenstown in the we

stories.title: Meet the man whose groundbreaking book is being devoured by the left
stories.summary: Anton Jäger has been lauded for his exceptional ability to make sense of these strange political times – and he’s only 32. The writer and academic explains why we’re living in an age of hyperpolitics ‘I am acutely conscious,” said Andy Burnham outside 10 Downing Street on Monday, “that I am the sixth person in the last 10 years to walk up this street.” It was good to hear him say it because, beneath all the cabinet gossip and rune-reading, there is a feeling of terror about the immediate future

stories.title: Oil prices near $100 a barrel after US attacks Iran and Houthis hit tankers in Red Sea – as it happened
stories.summary: Live, rolling coverage of business, economics and financial markets as traders fear attacks from Yemen and Iran creating ‘two-chokepoint problem’ The Houthi attacks off the coast of Yemen targeted Saudi Arabian tankers, but two Chinese vessels carrying Saudi oil appear to be trying to exit the Bab el-Mandeb strait, Reuters reports. stories.title: Risk of Parkinson’s disease may increase with prolonged exposure to road traffic noise
stories.summary: Pioneering study linking noise pollution to Parkinson’s finds 3% increase in risk for every 11.5dB rise in noise levels Long-term exposure to road traffic noise is associated with a higher risk of Parkinson’s disease, the biggest study of its kind has found. Parkinson’s disease is a progressive disorder in which parts of the brain become damaged over time, affecting movement and balance. Continue reading...

stories.title: Oil passes $100 a barrel again and shares slide as Middle East conflict escalates
stories.summary: Sharp rise in oil price reflects fears Houthi militias could strangle Saudi exports as US-Iran fighting intensifies How the Bab al-Mandab blockade threat pushed oil back above $100 The oil price broke above $100 a barrel for the first time in two months as a fresh escalation of the Middle East conflict threatens to compound disruption to global oil supplies. The benchmark oil price rose sharply on Thursday, from $95 the day before, amid fears that Yemen’s Houthi militia could strangle Saudi oil

stories.title: US-China AI rivalry heats up as Chinese models gain ground
stories.summary: China's AI platforms are rapidly closing the gap with their US counterparts, prompting fresh threats from the Trump administration. With accusations of intellectual property theft, could tensions boil over?

stories.title: EU fines Google €890 million over competition rules
stories.summary: The European Commission said it found that Google had preferenced its search engine and app store in ways that unfairly harmed competition.

stories.title: Lenacapavir will slow HIV infections if there's enough of it and it reaches at-risk groups
stories.summary: Lenacapavir is one injection, twice a year. It's a big improvement for people in high-risk groups, currently taking a pill a day against HIV infection. But its long-term impact depends on it reaching those very people.

stories.title: Nigeria: Armed 'bandits' kill more than 20 in Zamfara state
stories.summary: Armed gangs, known locally as "bandits," are a constant threat in northwestern and central Nigeria. They typically raid villages, steal livestock and kidnap residents for ransom.

stories.title: WAFCON 2026: What you need to know
stories.summary: Several months after it was supposed to start and with more teams than first planned, WAFCON 2026 is finally set to begin. The hosts want a maiden title while a tournament outsider have one of its best players.

stories.title: Canada-EU: Closer trade ties to counter Trump?
stories.summary: Given they share the world's longest land border, Canada and the US have historically enjoyed fruitful trade relations. But with Donald Trump escalating his tariff war, Canada is looking to the EU and further afield.

stories.title: ECB keeps interest rates unchanged amid Iran war inflation
stories.summary: The European Central Bank has kept its base interest rates steady but said it was "closely monitoring" the energy price shock. The impact of the war against Iran on energy costs has pushed inflation over ECB targets.

stories.title: Blocked by censors, China's animal lovers take fight against abuse offline and overseas
stories.summary: The killing of a dog and her puppies in Guangdong has sparked outrage inside and outside the country.

stories.title: Russia's businesses under strain from Ukraine's attacks on Wildberries
stories.summary: Ukrainian drones have hit several retail warehouses belonging to Russia's biggest online retailer Wildberries in a matter of days.

stories.title: Spanish police evacuate residents as wildfire threatens homes near Madrid
stories.summary: Guardia Civil officers evacuate residents in Villa del Prado, central Spain, as a fast-moving wildfire threatens homes near Toledo with flames and smoke.

stories.title: Divers restore Italy's Christ of the Abyss monument
stories.summary: Divers clean the Christ of the Abyss statue off San Fruttuoso, Italy, preserving the iconic underwater monument placed there in 1954.

stories.title: Gulf oil producers race to build alternatives to the Strait of Hormuz as oil hits $100
stories.summary: Gulf oil producers are spending billions of dollars on pipelines designed to route crude around the Strait of Hormuz, as Iran's grip on the waterway drags on causing oil prices to hit $100 a barrel for the first time since May.

stories.title: South Korea woman acquitted of murdering newborn after hospital delivery
stories.summary: The woman, who had wanted to end her pregnancy at 36 weeks, gave birth to a baby who doctors later killed.

stories.title: Giant swing offers breathtaking ride above the Swiss Alps
stories.summary: A giant swing opens at 2,110 metres on Switzerland's Grand Chamossaire peak, offering tourists an aerial ride above the Alps in Villars.

stories.title: Giant hot dog sculpture returns to New York Times Square
stories.summary: A giant hot dog sculpture returns to New York's Times Square as artists unveil a 65-foot installation marking America's 250th anniversary.

stories.title: 'Let's enjoy the ride' says Elon Musk, as AI fears mount
stories.summary: Talking to The Economist, Musk said he doesn't care if people hate him and said he was not a racist.

stories.title: Thousands evacuated as wildfire spreads in southwestern France
stories.summary: A wildfire spreads near Le Porge in Gironde, France, as firefighters battle flames across the forest and thousands of people are evacuated.

stories.title: Liquor store employee narrowly escapes leopard attack
stories.summary: After nearly five hours, officials captured the animal and it was later released back into the wild, local authorities confirmed.

stories.title: Trump's nuclear deal with Saudis jettisons longstanding US demands

stories.summary: Az alacsony vízállás miatt szemmel is jól látható egy tíz centi átmérőjű tárgy, ami robbanótest is lehet.

stories.title: A nullás gyógyszeráfa csak bemelegítés a sokkal többe kerülő lakossági adócsökkentésekhez
stories.summary: Szeptember 1-től jön a vényköteles gyógyszerek áfamentessége, a tűzifa áfacsökkentése pedig még a tél előtt. Az szja és a kata januártól változik, az egészséges élelmiszerek áfájára még nincs időpont.

stories.title: L. Simon László szerint penetráns volt a Fidesz kampánya
stories.summary: Navracsics Tibor szerint a 2015-ös migrációs válság óta radikálisan egyszerűsödött a Fidesz kommunikációja, az akadémisták helyett már kizárólag a kubikusoknak beszéltek, és a középosztály áprilisra azt érezte, hogy elvették előle a levegőt. L. Simon szerint megszűnt a kritika a Fideszben.

stories.title: NKA-ügy: Letartóztatták Hankó Balázs egykori kabinetfőnökét
stories.summary: Konczos Nóra az NKA-botrány hetedik gyanúsítottja, akit előzetesbe helyezett a bíróság. Ő kérte egy emailben azt, hogy biztosítsák a pénzt Mága Zoltán pályázatára, amiben 500 millió forintot szántak a cigány szavazók, az idősek és a politikailag bizonytalanok megszólítására.

stories.title: Marék Veronika: Hat évtizede kívülről tudom a Hamlet legszebb részeit
stories.summary: A József Attila-díjas meseírót tizenévesen sodorta el Shakespeare. A Boribon és az Annipanni-sorozat szerzője arról a drámáról mesélt, ami megváltoztatta az életét.

stories.summary: A Körös-vidéki Vízügyi Igazgatóság harmadfokú vízminőségi kárelhárítási készültséget rendelt el.

stories.summary: „Miközben jelentős társadalmi, addig költségvetési oldalon visszafogott hatása van” – közölte Kármán András.

stories.summary: Ukrajnában hatodik napja áll a gabona tengeri exportja. A háború ellenére is a világ 9. legnagyobb exportőrének hiánya a búza és a kukorica drágulásához vezethet, ahogyan négy évvel ezelőtt is történt az orosz blokád miatt.

stories.summary: Bongorral, Kolibrivel és Pálinkás Tamással, az Isten Háta Mögött frontemberével beszélgettünk a Fishingen. Az adásban szó esett függőségekről, józan alkotásról, a koncert utáni ürességről és arról is, hogy vajon tényleg minden zenész imposztorszindrómával küzd-e.

stories.title: Michelin-starred restaurant owner in South Korea faces jail over ant dessert
stories.summary: Prosecutors seek a one-year sentence and heavy fine after chef garnished dish with insects not approved for consumption Prosecutors in South Korea have asked a court to sentence the owner of a Michelin-starred restaurant to one year in prison for serving a dessert topped with ants, an insect not approved for consumption under local food safety law. stories.summary: Webcam footage shows the moment two trams collide on the Erasmus Bridge in the Dutch city. Officials said at least 15 people were injured, one seriously, in the collision that occurred on Wednesday Continue reading...

stories.title: Nicaragua’s congress announces ‘work plan’ to suspend elections
stories.summary: US secretary of state says international community should ‘join forces’ against authoritarian Ortega regime Nicaragua’s congress, controlled by the country’s long-serving president, Daniel Ortega, has announced a “work plan” to implement the authoritarian leader’s order that the country should no longer hold elections. The UN and the US strongly condemned Sunday’s announcement by Ortega, who has been in power since 2007 and, after a controversial constitutional change last year, now governs alon

stories.title: Feljelentést tettek az osztrák kőbányák az azbesztüggyel kapcsolatos kiszivárgott jelentések miatt
stories.summary: A cégek szerint jogtalanul juthattak a sajtóhoz és civilekhez az őket érintő jelentések.

stories.title: Lannert Judit kinevezte a Klebelsberg Központ új vezetőjét
stories.summary: Az oktatási és gyermekügyi miniszter szerint az új vezető célja, hogy rendszerszinten partneri viszony alakuljon ki az iskolákkal és a pedagógusokkal.

stories.title: Ezen a hétvégén is elmarad a Balaton-átúszás
stories.summary: Eredetileg az előző hétvégére tervezték, akkor a hidegfront miatt halasztották a versenyt.

stories.title: 890 millió eurós bírságot kapott a Google az Európai Bizottságtól
stories.summary: A vállalatot többek között azért bírságolták meg, mert jogellenesen előnyben részesítette a saját szolgáltatásait a keresőben.

stories.title: Egy Tiborczhoz köthető cég és a Szíjj László-féle Duna Aszfalt ügyében is feljelentést tett a kormány
stories.summary: Egy Tiborcz-közeli cég 59 milliárdot kapott. Feljelentést tettek az egyiptomi vasúti kocsik, a Zambia és Kongó közötti út, és az Észak-Macedóniának szánt hitel miatt is.

stories.title: A juniorokkal Eb-győztes Sótonyi László a férfi kézilabda-válogatott kapitánya
stories.summary: A 2028-as olimpiai részvételre halvány az esély, de új kapitánnyal próbálkozik a válogatott.

stories.title: Gyurkó Szilvia bízik abban, hogy ősszel már tartósan lecsökken a kórházban ragadt gyerekek száma
stories.summary: A gyermekjogi és gyermekvédelmi államtitkár nemcsak az ott ragadt gyerekek számának csökkentésén, hanem a jóllétükön is dolgozna.

stories.title: Szobanövény doktor, Balaton-parti műkincsek, népdalok és elektronika
stories.summary: Bevonódás, kikapcs és tökéletes randiprogramok. Néhány ötlet azoknak, akik az előttünk álló napokra keresnek feltöltő, lelassító rendezvényeket.

stories.title: Már át is vette a NEAK a Batthyány-Strattmann László Alapítvány korábbi feladatait
stories.summary: A drága és ritka kezelésekre vonatkozó kérelmekről már az egészségbiztosító dönt.

stories.title: Odüsszeuszi bolyongásra vállalkozik az, aki a legjobb verzióban nézné Christopher Nolan filmjét
stories.summary: A világon csak 41 olyan mozi van, ahol olyan formátumban nézhetjük az Odüsszeiát, ahogyan azt Nolan megálmodta. A rendező ezzel mintha szembemenne korábbi álláspontjával a mozik szerepéről.

stories.title: Can VR help you achieve your goals?
stories.summary: A Max Planck researcher has developed VR and smartphone tools that let you "meet" your future self. The science behind it is more powerful than it sounds.

stories.title: Turkey-PKK talks: Is peace finally possible?
stories.summary: A year ago, the Kurdish militant group PKK announced an end to its armed struggle. Since then, peace talks with Turkey have progressed slowly, but some observers believe they could succeed.

stories.title: Austria turns Hitler's birthplace into police station
stories.summary: The house where Adolf Hitler was born in 1889 is now a police station. Austrian authorities have long sought to prevent it becoming a neo-Nazi pilgrimage site.

stories.title: Will surge in German business bankruptcies hit growth?
stories.summary: Company insolvencies in Germany are rising sharply. Is this a sign of deeper economic weakness, or just a market correction that could ultimately benefit future growth?

stories.title: Germany: Merz replaces key figure following surrogacy row
stories.summary: Thorsten Frei, a close confidant of German Chancellor Friedrich Merz, is taking over the role as parliamentary group leader for the conservative CDU/CSU bloc. The position is a powerful one.

stories.title: How Russia is luring foreign nationals to fight in Ukraine
stories.summary: More and more, Russia is recruiting men from the world’s poorest countries to send them to the front in the Ukraine war. Why are people joining up?

stories.title: Trump says US has no interest in Iran talks as cost of war increases
stories.summary: The US entered its 11th consecutive night of strikes on Iran, while Tehran expanded its offensive against Gulf states. Meanwhile, Pakistan stepped up diplomatic efforts in hopes of salvaging the interim deal between the two.

stories.title: Outcry in Algeria over doctor deaths after gruelling on-call shifts
stories.summary: A resident neurosurgeon died of a heart attack after a 24-hour shift, and an anaesthesiologist died days later — deaths colleagues say reflect years of chronic overwork, understaffing and inadequate conditions in Algeria's public hospitals, demanding a revision of on-call rules dating back to 2013.

stories.title: Aerial footage reveals the scale of Spain’s La Mierla wildfire
stories.summary: Aerial footage shows the scale of the La Mierla wildfire in Guadalajara, Spain, as firefighters battle a blaze that has burned up to 30,000 hectares.

stories.title: Mamdani says NYC lacks authority to arrest Israeli PM Netanyahu
stories.summary: Netanyahu's office has dubbed the ICC a "kangaroo court" and called the arrest warrant against the Israeli leader "bogus."

stories.title: Zendaya's ancient Iranian earrings stir debate over stolen artefacts
stories.summary: Experts said the Iranian 3,000-year-old gold disc medallions Zendaya wore at a promo event for Christopher Nolan's Odyssey were "likely looted". Separately, a Zoroastrian symbol worn by IShowSpeed at the World Cup final has sparked further conversation about Persian cultural identity.

stories.title: 'It will get worse': World’s second-coldest capital faces record heat
stories.summary: Experts warn that the extreme heat reflects a broader climate trend that is increasing health, water and food security risks across Central Asia.

stories.title: Drones hit Wildberries warehouses in Russia for second week running
stories.summary: Fires broke out at Wildberries warehouse sites in Krasnodar and Stavropol after overnight drone attacks, injuring 15 people and forcing both facilities to suspend operations.

stories.title: Wreckage of Pan Am plane that shaped aviation safety found 74 years on
stories.summary: The deaths of 52 people on the Clipper Endeavor led to the introduction of mandatory pre-flight safety briefings.

stories.title: Watch: Louvre reopens gallery after jewellery heist
stories.summary: The Louvre Museum reopens its Apollo Gallery nine months after a robbery that shocked France.

stories.title: Congo's Ebola outbreak kills more than 1,000 people
stories.summary: The World Health Organization has said that the true scale of the outbreak could be two to four times larger than official figures indicate.

stories.title: A year after deadly jet crash at Bangladesh school, families demand answers
stories.summary: This week marks one year since a military jet struck a school in Dhaka, killing 36, most of them children.

stories.title: Watch: What we know about the US-Saudi nuclear deal
stories.summary: The BBC's Sarah Smith explains the implications of the landmark cooperation agreement between both countries.

stories.title: Even camels can't cope: Africa's ships of the desert hit by rising temperatures
stories.summary: Herders and experts tell the BBC how Africa's camels are suffering because of extreme heat.

stories.title: Canada's 'powerful' dairy sector is in Trump's trade crosshairs
stories.summary: Canada's industry is in the spotlight after the US president singled it out as one of three main irritants used to justify new tariffs.

stories.title: Germany news: Merz hosts Azerbaijan President Aliyev
stories.summary: Talks between the German chancellor and Ilham Aliyev have focused on energy. Meanwhile, Interior Minister Alexander Dobrindt will present a report on sexual offenses against children.

stories.title: Iran's IRGC claims attack on Amazon's main data hub in Bahrain
stories.summary: The IRGC claimed its cruise missiles destroyed Amazon's central data infrastructure in Bahrain and hit US air defence systems. Bahraini, US and Amazon officials have not responded.

stories.title: Lebanese army begins deployment to southern regions after Israel deal
stories.summary: Beirut announced the beginning of its mobilisation to the southern Lebanese regions as President Aoun prepares for a high-stakes meeting with US President Donald Trump on Tuesday to conclude his four-day visit to Washington.

stories.title: UN reports 144 dead or missing after migrant boat is stranded at sea for 25 days
stories.summary: Two children onboard the boat bound for the Canary Islands lost their entire family before the passengers were picked up by a rescue operation from Mauritania.

stories.title: Trump promises US support for Lebanon in White House meeting with president
stories.summary: Lebanon and Israel have held rare direct talks mediated by Washington. Aoun’s visit was the first to the White House by a Lebanese president since 2009.

stories.title: Healthy diet too expensive for one in three people globally, UN report finds
stories.summary: Global hunger declined for the third year in a row in 2025 but experts call for action to bring down costs of fruit, vegetables and dairy as report shows 2.69bn people can’t afford to eat well One in three people cannot afford to eat a healthy diet, a new report has found, prompting calls for urgent action on food affordability. There are 2.69 billion people unable to afford a diverse diet that meets their energy and nutrient needs, and it will take “immense” amounts of work to achieve the globa

stories.title: Rescuers in Afghanistan search for missing and dead after flash floods destroy homes
stories.summary: National disaster agency confirms ‘severe financial and human losses’ in eastern province of Nuristan Rescue crews in Afghanistan are searching for survivors and bodies among the rubble of houses destroyed by devastating flash floods that authorities said had killed nearly two dozen people and left more than 100 people missing. The Afghanistan national disaster management authority said on Tuesday that 23 people had died, 80 had been injured and more than 100 were missing after floods struck the
